page: Microsoft Azure FX96mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 1832.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 19.08 GB per vCPU.

For local storage needs, this server includes 5669 GB of bundled local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no integrated graphics processing units on this instance type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX96mds_v2 offers high memory density and substantial local storage capacity relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads. The lack of hardware acceleration limits its utility for GPU-bound tasks. This configuration is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure FX96mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server within the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is equipped with 1832.0 GB of system memory, providing 19.08 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 5669 GB of bundled local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations. It does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This high resource density makes the server qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The hardware profile is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory data processing, and heavy transactional workloads that require substantial memory capacity and local storage without the need for hardware-accelerated graphics processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX96mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 1832 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, designed for large-scale database and in-memory workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive x86_64 server platform featuring ninety-six dedicated cores, substantial system memory, and large bundled local storage for enterprise database workloads.
